your_book:
Friday's session was essentially a wash for Jaleel's book — unrealized gain of $1.72 on a $21,614 portfolio tells you the day's crosscurrents nearly cancelled each other out. The standout mover was NVDA, up 4.03% to $1,376.66, the clearest beneficiary of whale-driven IT momentum and ongoing AI-capex optimism; that's meaningful even at a 6.4% weight. DHR added 1.57% and LULU recovered 2.36% — notable given LULU's guidance-cut baggage, though one bounce doesn't rehabilitate a name with five straight negative Americas comps. On the other side, GILD was the session's worst performer, down 3.72% to $412.86, compounding an existing −2.5% open loss on a name already under drug-pricing policy pressure. RKLB slid another 1.83%, deepening its −17.8% open loss — the widest in the book — on a QARP of just 39.6 and an AVOID verdict. VRTX and NBIX also gave back 2.24% and 1.91% respectively despite being in-the-money; no headline catalyst, just sector drag.

The sharpest thesis-vs-price divergence remains ADBE: rated STRONG BUY with a QARP of 73, yet sitting −8.8% in the hole. Today it gained 0.44% alongside a Guggenheim note pushing back on AI-disruption fears, but that's noise around a stubborn gap between fundamentals (beat-and-raise in June) and a market narrative fixated on Claude Design and the CFO exit. BSX is a similar story — STRONG BUY, QARP 82.2, but −6.5% unrealized and carrying a guidance slash (FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5%) with Q2 earnings due July 29. ZTS bucked the drift today, up 0.64%, but remains −4.1% on an 11.5% weight; animal-health cycle softness hasn't gone away.

Watch the earnings calendar closely: ABT reports July 16, NOW on July 22, BSX on July 29, and the pharma cluster faces a drug-pricing policy update around July 30. RKLB continues to demand a hard look — it carries the largest loss, the lowest quality score in the book, and an AVOID verdict. Cash is effectively zero at $68, so any repositioning requires selling something.

attention.headline: Largest loss, lowest QARP, AVOID verdict — no thesis support
attention.note: RKLB is down 17.8% with a QARP of 39.6 and an explicit AVOID rating; it dropped another 1.83% today and there is no analytical case in the data for holding a 7.1% weight here.

attention.headline: 3.72% single-session drop on an already-underwater name with policy overhang
attention.note: Gilead is −2.5% open, carries a HOLD-QUAL verdict, a QARP of 64.6, and is directly named in the MFN drug-pricing deal flag — today's drop may be the policy pressure beginning to reprice the stock.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, Q2 earnings July 29 — elevated flag with a tight window
attention.note: BSX is rated STRONG BUY but carries an elevated risk flag (FY26 growth cut, management guiding flat through Q3) and reports in 19 days; the −6.5% open loss could widen sharply on a miss or further cut.

attention.headline: Narrative keeps overriding fundamentals — thesis drift widening at 16.2% weight
attention.note: ADBE is the book's largest position, rated STRONG BUY with QARP 73, yet sits −8.8% as AI-disruption fear persists despite a beat-and-raise; today's 0.44% Guggenheim bounce is thin comfort given the next catalyst isn't until September 10.
